body:
## Automated review — input-validation PR

This is a large refactoring that goes well beyond the stated scope of "input validation." Below are concrete issues found in the diff.

comments.body: Engine created at module level with `echo=True`. This leaks every SQL query to stdout — including potentially sensitive data — in all environments. Make `echo` configurable (e.g. via env var).
`except BaseException` catches `SystemExit`, `KeyboardInterrupt`, and `GeneratorExit`. Use `except Exception` instead to avoid swallowing system signals.
Type hint `list["UserModel"]` is wrong — this relationship stores `SSHKeyModel` instances (foreign key points to `ssh_key`). Should be `list["SSHKeyModel"]`.
`session.delete(user)` passes a domain `User` object not tracked by this session. This will silently fail or raise. Should be `session.delete(model)` using the ORM instance queried on line 27.
`~/.ssh/authorized_keys` does not exist after `useradd -m` — that file is only created later in `_add_ssh_key`. This `chmod` command will fail at user-creation time.
`self.user.ssh_keys.remove(self)` is called here and again on line 41. The second call will raise `ValueError` since the element was already removed.
Mutable default argument `[]` is shared across all `User` instances. Use `None` and default to an empty list inside the method body.
